stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains, including transformers and text-generation.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, secure model deployment practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and performance tracking for a significant open-source LLM variant.

stories.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: NASA has selected Relativity Space for a 2028 mission to Mars.
The mission involves launching NASA's Aeolus payload to Mars.
Relativity Space will manage the spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations under a new public-private partnership.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has identified a new, lightweight backdoor malware dubbed 'Crypto Clipper'.
The malware is capable of stealing cryptocurrency from infected systems.
It spreads via USB drives and utilizes the Tor network for communication.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: The research identifies that fine-tuning language models on insecure code can cause 'emergent misalignment' related to poorly understood internal structures.
The study proposes and validates an 'actionable activation-space direction' that can causally separate aligned from misaligned states across multiple model architectures.
This direction achieved a high separation rate (99.6%) across four different instruction-tuned model families (Qwen2.5, Gemma-2, Llama-3.2, Ministral-3).

stories.title: Hyundai buys Boston Dynamics
stories.author: ck2
bullet_points: Hyundai has acquired Boston Dynamics, a major robotics firm.
This represents a significant strategic move into advanced robotics and automation.
The acquisition signals a major industry shift towards integrating sophisticated robotics into manufacturing and other sectors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model outputs and faster response times.
The updated engine also demonstrates reduced memory consumption when running models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI capabilities on Mac.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and adoption.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: The FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of 'agency drama,' notably involving a Trump official refusing review.
This signifies a major regulatory milestone for mRNA technology in the vaccine market.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for defense purposes.
The increased drone spending is noted as potentially boosting Taiwanese business overseas.
The context of the news is heightened geopolitical tension involving China.

stories.title: Android verification is coming: Google confirms timeline and supported app stores
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is implementing a new Android verification system service this month.
Significant changes related to this verification are scheduled to begin in September.
The update addresses changes concerning supported app stores on Android.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses the static nature of state-of-the-art flow-matching Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that allows pronunciation corrections without retraining the core model.
Adaptation is achieved by learning pronunciation corrections as latent conditioning edits in the text embedding space.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces 'StylisticBias,' a new benchmark to evaluate attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in MLLM research by separating appearance effects from inherent identity differences when models judge people.
Focuses on how subtle visual cues influence social biases within increasingly consequential AI deployments.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: Proposes a novel method to align Large Language Models (LLMs) using implicit user feedback instead of relying solely on expensive explicit human feedback.
Addresses two major limitations in current LLM alignment techniques: the scarcity of explicit feedback and the failure to utilize valuable implicit user signals.
Suggests a path to make LLM alignment more scalable and economically viable by leveraging real-world user behavior, similar to how internet giants operate.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically benefiting users on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning.
It is optimized for complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of subagents.
Web search functionality is now available within the Claude Code environment via Ollama.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machine.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Hue’s wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Philips Hue introduced wired wall modules to integrate non-smart lights into its ecosystem.
The new modules are designed to be installed behind existing wall switches.
Hue also launched more affordable versions of its Signe series lamps and upgraded its E14 bulbs.

stories.title: NASA asks Northrop Grumman to stop working on lunar HALO module
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has directed Northrop Grumman to cease work on the lunar HALO module.
The decision involves reassigning the majority of the affected employees to other ongoing programs.
The news indicates a shift or change in NASA's lunar exploration priorities or contracts.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The research investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in AI.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity regarding how individual words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It introduces cross-attention attribution, adapting the DAAM framework to the speech domain for the first time, to analyze this influence.
The method generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers of speech diffusion models to improve controllability in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents a structured task state.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where task state (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving reliability for agents in complex domains like customer service that require maintaining state across interactions and tool calls.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: Introduces DeepSWIP, a new framework designed to enable counterfactual reasoning in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog.
Addresses the limitation of standard inference in these systems by providing a single-world counterfactual semantics.
Achieves this by using neural materialization to translate fixed-context neural predicates into standard ProbLog choices, allowing for Single World Intervention Programs (SWIPs).

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: SARLO-80 introduces a new, large-scale dataset designed to bridge the gap between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data.
The dataset addresses limitations in existing SAR-optical resources, which often lack complex-valued measurements and native acquisition geometry.
This development is crucial for advancing physically grounded multimodal learning in AI, particularly for high-resolution remote sensing applications.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of LiveCodeBench (LCB) to support multiple programming languages.
LCB is an established benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation, known for its contamination-aware evaluation.
The research addresses a key limitation of LCB, which was previously restricted solely to Python.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Proposes 'privacy via predictability' as an alternative to traditional Differential Privacy (DP).
This new framework offers a fine-grained privacy measure that accounts for the attacker's specific prior knowledge.
It aims to mitigate the rigid, worst-case privacy-accuracy trade-off inherent in standard DP mechanisms.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, contrasting with standard high-throughput KV caching.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' for checkpointing and restoring the full execution state, not just the KV cache.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require rapid state management under tight latency budgets.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical gap in LLM coding agents: the lack of high-level operational knowledge about a software repository.
The paper investigates the effectiveness of using auxiliary context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide coding agents.
It proposes a 'Probe-and-Refine' tuning method to improve agent performance by better integrating repository guidance.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where state (facts, constraints) must be reconstructed from the prompt across turns.
Focuses on improving policy-adherent tool-calling agents, particularly relevant for complex domains like customer service.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale bilingual dataset (1.2M pairs) of CT/MR images and text for radiology.
Presents RefRad2D, which features automatically generated VQA and spatial grounding subsets using LLM curation.
The resulting model, RadGrounder, is designed for scalable training of vision-language models in radiology, performing tasks like report generation and VQA.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a common challenge in online content moderation.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for training LLMs.
Aims to improve the generation of effective, non-repetitive counterspeech responses against harmful online exchanges.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-operations-oriented inference optimization for large language models (LLMs).
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
The research aims to improve the scalability, cost-efficiency, and stability of large model services.

stories.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
stories.author: Chanjin Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces PsyScore, a framework designed to improve Automated Essay Scoring (AES) by integrating psychometric principles.
Addresses the limitation of existing systems where scoring and feedback are treated separately, often lacking learner-specific context.
Aims to provide both reliable assessment and actionable, proficiency-level-aware instructional feedback using LLMs.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF) for analyzing Nigerian public discourse.
MIF is a nine-dimension schema designed to differentiate true communicative intent from surface sentiment.
Argues that AI failures in Nigerian languages stem from context failure, not just translation issues, challenging existing sentiment benchmarks.

stories.title: AI Model Failover Drills: Keep Agents Useful When Providers Break
stories.author: jackm-singularity
bullet_points: The article discusses proactive strategies for ensuring AI agent reliability during external provider outages.
Key techniques mentioned include schema-safe retries, fallback contracts, and circuit breakers.
The focus is on implementing robust failover drills and logging to maintain service continuity.
